task_completion.visual_evidence: The JStock application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Locate and select NVIDIA (NVDA) in JStock.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The stock ticker 'NVDA' is visible and highlighted or selected in the JStock interface (e.g., in the watchlist).

task_completion.description: Open the historical stock price chart for NVDA.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A chart window or tab displaying historical stock prices for NVDA is visible within the JStock application.

task_completion.description: Initiate the process to save or export the chart image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save', 'Save As', or 'Export' dialog window is open on top of the JStock application.

task_completion.description: Save the chart image as NVDA_chart.png to the /home/ga/Documents/ folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog displays 'NVDA_chart.png' in the file name field and '/home/ga/Documents/' (or the 'Documents' folder) as the target directory, or a file manager shows the saved file in the correct location.

integrity.description: The agent used the JStock graphical interface to export the chart.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with JStock menus or right-click context menus to save the chart, rather than using terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent exported the chart properly rather than taking a full-screen screenshot.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses JStock's built-in save/export functionality instead of a screenshot tool or terminal command (e.g., scrot).

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to artificially create or move the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are visible showing commands like 'touch', 'cp', 'mv', or 'wget' to bypass the GUI export process.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is /home/ga/Documents/NVDA_chart.png. The exported image must be in PNG format and have a file size greater than 5000 bytes. The image dimensions must not be exactly 1920x1080 to ensure it is a properly exported chart and not just a full-screen screenshot.
